matrix_label: M5 Companion, Incident, Sync, Residency, and Offboarding Matrix

lane_rows.scope_summary: Browser/mobile companion notifications for build, review, and agent events — read-only with no editor authority

locality_disclosure.stays_local: Notification source events are computed by the local core and stay inspectable offline.
locality_disclosure.staged: Companion push fan-out to paired browser/mobile sessions rolls out per cohort.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Paired companion delivery requires the companion relay; the local core never depends on it to function.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Companion review/approve follow-up: inspect findings and approve or defer pre-staged actions without authoring edits

locality_disclosure.stays_local: Review content and decision records are authored and stored by the local core.
locality_disclosure.staged: Companion-initiated approvals roll out behind staged cohorts.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Relaying an approval to a running desktop session requires the companion relay and an active host.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Companion session-follow and handoff eligibility: observe an active desktop session and resume context, narrow to read plus handoff

locality_disclosure.stays_local: Session state and handoff records originate on the local core and survive offline.
locality_disclosure.staged: Session-follow streaming is staged per cohort and capability.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Live follow requires an active host session and the companion relay; eligibility narrows when either is unavailable.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Companion light-edit: bounded text touch-ups relayed to the host for preview/approval, never a full mobile editor

locality_disclosure.stays_local: Edits apply only through the host's local change pipeline with preview and revert.
locality_disclosure.staged: Light-edit is early-access behind an explicit capability gate.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Applying a light-edit requires an active host session and the companion relay; without them it is read-only.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Attributable incident workspaces binding crash trails, evidence spans, and runbook steps to a redacted support bundle preview

locality_disclosure.stays_local: Incident evidence, missing-span facts, and runbook records are local-first and inspectable offline.
locality_disclosure.staged: Cross-device incident sharing rolls out per cohort.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Escalation to a managed support channel requires admin continuity; local triage never depends on it.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Inspectable managed sync of settings, profile, and device registry with conflict review and no silent server authority

locality_disclosure.stays_local: The local core is the source of truth; every synced record stays inspectable and reconcilable offline.
locality_disclosure.staged: Managed sync expands per cohort and record class.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Server-side sync and conflict relay require the sync provider and, for managed tenants, admin continuity.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Provable customer-managed-key and end-to-end-encryption residency posture for managed/synced artifacts, with region pinning

locality_disclosure.stays_local: Local-only artifacts never leave the device and carry no residency dependency.
locality_disclosure.staged: Customer-managed-key and region pinning roll out per managed tenant.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: End-to-end-encryption and region-residency guarantees require the managed key authority and admin continuity, and are claimed only when verifiable.

lane_rows.scope_summary: Offboarding that exports user-owned durable artifacts and guarantees the local core keeps working after any managed teardown

locality_disclosure.stays_local: User-owned local work and history remain on the device and fully usable after offboarding.
locality_disclosure.staged: Bulk managed-export tooling rolls out per cohort.
locality_disclosure.requires_provider_or_admin_continuity: Revoking managed/cloud artifacts requires admin continuity; local-core continuity is never gated on it.
